Notice Of Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Apr-27-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
William Bunting III, Esq. 415-617-0244 for Petitioner.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested/Discussed. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER BELFOR USA GROUP INC'S Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award.

The unopposed "petition to confirm contractual arbitration award" is granted.

Order and Judgment are signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Nov-03-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER BELFOR USA GROUP INC'S Petition To Compel Arbitration:

Petitioner Belfor USA Group Inc.'s petition for an order compelling completion of a pending arbitration is denied. The Court lacks jurisdiction over the parties to the contractual arbitration, which has been pending since 2019. Any delays or other issues that arise in the arbitration must be addressed by the arbitrator. "An arbitration has a life of its own outside the judicial system. The trial court may not step into a case submitted to arbitration and tell the arbitrator what to do and when to do it." (Titan/Value Equities Group, Inc. v. Superior Court (1994) 29 Cal.App.4th 482, 487-489 [holding trial court exceeded its jurisdiction when it attempted to remedy arbitration delay by ordering arbitration to proceed under stated conditions subject to reinstatement on trial calendar, rejecting contention that "the court has power to intervene on behalf of parties stalled in arbitration"]; see also Briggs v. Resolution Remedies (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 1395, 1400 ["Once a dispute is submitted to arbitration, the [California Arbitration Act] contemplates limited, if any, judicial involvement. 'Typically, those who enter into arbitration agreements expect that their dispute will be resolved without necessity for any contact with the courts. It is the job of the arbitrator, not the court, to resolve all questions needed to determine the controversy." (citations omitted)]; Brock v. Kaiser Foundation Hospitals (1992) 10 Cal.App.4th 1790, 1808 [it is up to the arbitrator, and not the court, to grant relief for delay in bringing an arbitration to resolution].)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
